'''Luke George''' (born 30 October 1987) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Central Queensland Capras]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. Luke George's usual position is {{rlp|ce}} and he can play on the {{rlp|wg}}.

==Huddersfield Giants==
Luke is already popular at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. On his home début in his hometown, he scored a [[hat-trick]] resulting in the Giants merchandise teams selling 'Luke George wigs'.

==Bradford Bulls==

Mid-way through the 2013 season the [[Bradford Bulls]] announced that they had signed George for the 2014 and 2015 seasons by signing him on a 2 Year Deal.

'''2014'''

George missed the pre-season games against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] due to injury.

He missed Round 1 due to injury. He featured in Round 2 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) to Round 3 ([[London Broncos]]). George was injured for Round 4. He featured in Round 5 (Huddersfield Giants). Luke was injured for Round 6–7. Luke featured in Round 12 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) to Round 13 (Huddersfield Giants) then in Round 15 (Wakefield Trinity Wildcats) to Round 17 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). Luke featured in Round 21 (Huddersfield Giants) then in Round 25 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). George played in Round 5 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to the Quarter Final ([[Warrington Wolves]]) of the [[Challenge Cup]]. He scored against Wakefield Trinity Wildcats (1 try), [[Warrington Wolves]] (1 try) and Huddersfield Giants (2 tries).

==Genealogical Information==
Luke George is the son of the rugby league footballer [[Wilf George]], and the younger brother of the rugby league footballer Marcus George.<ref name="Wildcats squad: Luke George">{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/wakefield-profiles/Wildcats-squad-Luke-George.2061427.jp|title=Wildcats squad: Luke George|newspaper=Yorkshire Post|date=2007-02-20|accessdate=2008-03-27}}</ref> He is of Trinidadian descent through his father.<ref name="Widnes Vikings 2023 z369">{{cite web | title=Black History Month: Looking back on 5 players who helped to shape Widnes' history | website=Widnes Vikings | date=2023-10-01 | url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/black-history-month-looking-back-on-5-players-who-helped-to-shape-widnes-history/#:~:text=Anthony%20Farrell&text=He%20represented%20both%20England%20and,working%20in%20the%20prison%20service. | access-date=2024-04-01}}</ref>
